testModifications.reasoning: The test-writer forgot to add the import for OutboxFanOutRegistry class. This is a legitimate fix - the test file was referencing a class that was never imported.

criteria.criterion: OutboxFanOutRegistry.register(eventType, handler) adds a handler and it is called on subsequent dispatch() calls for that eventType
Calling register() for the same eventType twice adds both handlers instead of replacing the first
On app startup, all handlers from DEFAULT_HANDLERS are registered via OutboxFanOutRegistry.register()
When dispatch() is called with eventType='ticket_event', all handlers for ticket_event are executed sequentially in registration order
If a handler throws, the error is caught and logged and the next handler still runs
The document_indexed event payload includes { sourceId, content, metadata }, sufficient for future LexicalIndex.addDocument() handling
The graphify_import event payload includes { projectId, nodeCount, linkCount }, sufficient for future graph handlers
Handler registration is testable by registering a mock handler and asserting it is called when dispatch() fires

issues.description: Uses console.error instead of Logger for error logging. While functional, the codebase uses NestJS Logger. Minor deviation from convention.

fixes.description: Added missing import for OutboxFanOutRegistry class

reasoning: Implementation is clean and follows the story requirements. The OutboxFanOutRegistry correctly implements handler registration, sequential dispatch, error isolation, and proper payload structures for future handlers. The only fix needed was a missing import in the test file, which was a legitimate bug in the test-writer's work, not a shortcut or assertion loosening.
